summary: Gruber issues a formal correction to his earlier piece 'Richard Stallman's Disgrace,' in which he included an anecdote from a reader alleging Stallman made sexual advances at VA Linux. Zed Shaw and others on Twitter pointed out that the details — working at VA Linux, having an open marriage — matched Eric S. Raymond, not Stallman. Gruber's source confirmed the mix-up, having conflated the two 'TLA Old Nerds.' Gruber takes full responsibility for publishing the error without sufficient fact-checking and has updated the original article.
key_points: A reader's anecdote about sexual harassment at VA Linux was mistakenly attributed to Richard Stallman when it actually described Eric S. Raymond
Zed Shaw identified the error by noting Stallman never worked at VA Linux, was never married, and wouldn't have used the term 'open source'
Gruber acknowledges he should have caught the factual inconsistencies himself before publishing
The original source confirmed the mix-up, admitting he conflated ESR and RMS over the years
Gruber takes sole responsibility for the error rather than deflecting blame to his source
money_quotes: I should have known Stallman would never have worked with VA Linux (he'd have insisted upon it being named 'VA GNU/Linux', and likely would have had no interest in what was a very commercial enterprise no matter what its name).
It was my fault and mine alone for publishing it.
I guess I assumed there were not two creepy guys talking about free and open software.
I sincerely and deeply regret the error.
His former colleague at VA Linux, the woman who was propositioned by Raymond, surely remembers it clearly.
key_insight: Gruber models thorough journalistic accountability by issuing a detailed public correction, taking full personal responsibility for publishing an anecdote he should have fact-checked against readily available information.
